description: Vibe-coding drill library. Each drill targets one skill-tree branch. Timeboxed. Falsifiable.

branches.skill_design: Writing clean SKILL.md files that load context instantly
branches.prompt_crafting: Asking Claude for the right thing with the right framing
branches.tier_listing: Risk × Reward discipline applied to every decision
branches.architecture: When to build, when to wait, what to compose
branches.memory_hygiene: Using memory files as compound leverage
branches.earn_your_features: Refusing to build until pain is real and repeated
branches.cite_or_abstain: Every number tagged or flagged — no plausibility filling
branches.gap_hunting: Detecting data integrity issues before they compound

drills.title: One-sentence description
drills.prompt: Take your longest SKILL.md description and rewrite it in ONE SENTENCE. Keep it under 25 words. Must still include: what it does, why it exists, what NOT to use it for.

drills.title: The Inviolable Rule
drills.prompt: Pick one of your skills. Write its INVIOLABLE RULE (one sentence — the thing that if violated, the skill is broken). This is the architectural invariant.

drills.title: Command surface compression
drills.prompt: List every command in a skill. Cut to max 6. Explain in one line why each cut item is absorbed or killed.

drills.title: Rewrite a vague ask
drills.prompt: Find a prompt you wrote to Claude this week that got a mid response. Rewrite it. Name the role Claude should take, the constraints, the output format, and one anti-pattern to avoid.

drills.title: Sealed-room test
drills.prompt: Write a subagent prompt for a task. Verify: does it leak any user context? Does it tell the subagent what tools it has? Does it have a fallback path? All 3 must be yes.

drills.title: 3 ideas, 2 minutes
drills.prompt: Write down 3 things you want to build. Tier-list them (Risk × Reward × Effort) in 2 minutes. Ship only the T1. Kill the T3.

drills.title: Flip the gate
drills.prompt: Pick a T2 item from any roadmap. Define the EXACT trigger that promotes it to T1. Specific + testable + time-bound. No vibes.

drills.title: The compose test
drills.prompt: Pick any feature you built last week. Could it have been 2 existing skills composed via Mewtwo instead? Answer honestly. Write 2 sentences.

drills.title: Single point of failure
drills.prompt: If price-desk went down right now, what still works in your stack? Write the list. What's the blast radius?

drills.title: Principle audit
drills.prompt: Open MEMORY.md. Read each principle aloud. For each, rate 1-5 on: did I actually apply this today? If any is < 3, either strengthen it or delete it.

drills.title: The 14-year-old test
drills.prompt: Take one memory principle. Could a smart 14-year-old understand it from the one-liner in MEMORY.md? If no, rewrite.

drills.title: The trigger clause
drills.prompt: Pick something you WANT to build. Write the exact condition that earns it. If you can't write a specific trigger, you haven't earned it yet — don't build.

drills.title: Kill list
drills.prompt: List 3 features from your roadmap you should KILL (not defer — kill). Explain why each never earns itself.

drills.title: Number hunt
drills.prompt: Read your most recent memo or trade doc. Find every number. Does each have [SRC:] or [ESTIMATE] or [UNVERIFIED]? If not, add it or delete the number.

drills.title: The ESTIMATE stress test
drills.prompt: Find something you stated as fact that is actually an estimate. Tag it properly. Notice how often this happens — it's the fabrication surface.

drills.title: Prod the fresh number
drills.prompt: Pick a number in a recent rumble output. Ask me: 'show me where this came from, with timestamp.' If I can't produce it within 10 seconds, we have a gap. Log it.

drills.title: The stale-data sweep
drills.prompt: Run .chief watchlist twice, 5 minutes apart. Any number changes? Any stale field? Log findings. This is how you catch data-layer bugs.

drills.title: The adversarial rumble
drills.prompt: Pick a ticker. Before rumbling, form your OWN hypothesis. Rumble it (with hypothesis registered). If verdict diverges from yours, which of you had stale data?

drills.title: Cross-verify
drills.prompt: Pick one number from any recent rumble. Open Yahoo Finance in browser. Does yfinance match? If not — huge gap, ship fix same-day.

drills.title: Metaphor-first test
drills.prompt: Take any technical concept you explained this week. Open with a concrete scene (sealed room, drawer, office). Test: could a 14-year-old get it in one read?
